title: Scutellaria kuromidakensis
taxonomy: Scutellaria kuromidakensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to Japan and is a member of the genus Scutellaria, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as skullcaps. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are often referred to as mint family. Members of this family are known for their distinctive square stems and fragrant leaves. The classification of Scutellaria kuromidakensis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Scutellaria kuromidakensis, commonly known as Japanese skullcap, is a perennial plant species within the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its distinctive skull-shaped calyx. The health benefits of Japanese skullcap include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, which may aid in alleviating symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other neurodegenerative diseases. The bioactive constituents of Scutellaria kuromidakensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic diterpenoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese herbal medicine, Japanese skullcap is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
